title: cerastium fontanum subsp. lucorum
intro: Cerastium fontanum subsp. lucorum is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae, commonly known as the mouse-ear chickweed, which is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the genus Cerastium. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Cerastium fontanum subsp. lucorum include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a potential natural remedy for various skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Cerastium fontanum subsp. lucorum can be used to make teas, infusions, and topical applications, such as creams and salves, to harness its medicinal properties.
